inputs.problem:
A manufacturer was losing product to foaming defects - rework, scrap, and no clear answer on why.
We took their shopfloor data and within one week had identified which parameters were driving the defects and what to change.
Immediate result: 20% defect reduction. No months-long study. No disruption to the line. One week.

prospects.name: LG Energy Solution
prospects.description: High-volume battery manufacturing with recurring quality issues in electrode and cell assembly processes.
prospects.why: CMA’s rapid parameter optimization can reduce defect rates without disrupting continuous production lines.

prospects.name: Pfizer Injectable Manufacturing
prospects.description: Precision vial filling lines with occasional misfill defects leading to costly product loss.
prospects.why: CMA’s AI-guided correlation analysis can detect and prevent fill errors early in the process.

prospects.name: Tesla Gigafactory
prospects.description: Complex automated assembly processes for EV batteries with yield losses due to process instability.
prospects.why: CMA’s solution can quickly identify key process parameters impacting yield and improve throughput.

prospects.name: Siemens Healthineers Diagnostic Equipment Production
prospects.description: Precision assembly of diagnostic devices with intermittent defects linked to environmental and machine parameters.
prospects.why: CMA’s correlation modeling can isolate problem parameters in complex assembly processes.

prospects.name: Johnson Controls
prospects.description: Mass production of industrial batteries with scrap losses due to unpredictable foaming and sealing defects.
prospects.why: CMA’s rapid defect-driver identification can reduce scrap rates and improve profitability.

domains.application: Optimize parameters in precise liquid filling processes to reduce under/overfill defects.
domains.adaptation: Integrate with cleanroom regulatory compliance and GMP data handling.

domains.application: Identify process variables causing coating defects and optimize without production downtime.
domains.adaptation: Adapt for high variability due to environmental factors and multi-stage coating.

domains.application: Correlate production parameters with microscopic yield defects for rapid process tuning.
domains.adaptation: Integrate with extremely high precision and cleanroom environment constraints.

domains.application: Detect and mitigate fill-level inconsistencies and contamination risks in bottling lines.
domains.adaptation: Adjust models for variable viscosity and seasonal ingredient changes.

domains.application: Analyze curing and layup parameters to reduce structural defects in composite parts.
domains.adaptation: Adapt to low-volume, high-cost production with stringent certification requirements.

matrix.rationale: Both involve high-precision filling processes where CMA’s model can rapidly detect defect drivers.
Paint defects impact quality and throughput; CMA can optimize parameters but environmental variability is high.
Direct match to CMA’s proven foaming defect solution with clear scalability potential.
High-precision defect analysis is applicable, but adaptation to semiconductor-specific processes is needed.
Similar foaming defect problems make CMA’s approach immediately applicable.
Fill-level optimization is relevant, but food and beverage industry has different regulatory and process dynamics.
CMA’s parameter optimization could reduce costly defects, but production is low-volume and highly customized.
